india_fit.price_band_reason: Cape kurtis can be produced within ₹399–₹1,499 range with basic fabric and minimal construction complexity.
india_fit.climate_fit_reason: Capes add fabric bulk unsuitable for Indian heat, though lightweight fabrics and monsoon layering could offset this partially.
india_fit.cultural_fit_reason: Cape kurtis maintain modesty norms while adding contemporary silhouette appeal that aligns with festive and occasion wear.
india_fit.value_fashion_fit_reason: Cape construction adds perceived style and visual drama at low cost, but mainstream value-fashion shoppers (Meesho, Vishal Mega Mart) still prioritize comfort and ease-of-wear over novelty silhouettes.

claude_synthesis.convergence_summary: Cape kurtis show online/social interest but zero marketplace traction and zero Google search volume in India, suggesting a niche aspirational trend that has not yet converted to mainstream search or shopping behavior.
claude_synthesis.bet_reasoning: Google Trends flatline (0/100) and Myntra/Meesho absence are strong negative signals—this is not a live commercial trend in value-fashion retail right now. The 10 web-indexed social mentions indicate content exists but has not driven measurable search or purchase intent, meaning early-stage or influencer-driven interest without mass demand.
claude_synthesis.skepticism_flag: The 10 web-indexed social mentions may overrepresent niche fashion blogs or influencer posts that reach small engaged audiences; they do not indicate whether value-fashion customers are actively searching for or buying cape kurtis at scale.
